global_scroll.headline: Fed holds rate at 3.50–3.75% under new Chair Warsh; dot plot signals hawkish tilt with nine members favouring a hike
RBI holds repo rate at 5.25% unanimously; maintains neutral stance, revises FY27 GDP forecast down to 6.6%
ECB hikes rates by 25bps to 2.25% — first increase since Sep 2023 amid Middle East energy inflation
US-Iran military escalation renews Strait of Hormuz supply fears; Brent crude rises above $73
India GCC count crosses 2,100 centers; AI-first operating models driving product ownership beyond cost arbitrage
Indian SaaS ecosystem crosses $50B ARR milestone; vertical SaaS and PLG strategies dominate growth
FIIs record ₹45,121 Cr net selling in June 2026; DII SIP inflows at ₹30,954 Cr cushion market
Indian mutual fund AUM hits record ₹81.58 lakh crore; SIPs now 21% of total industry assets
India AI services revenue reaches $10–12 billion as 25% of tech firms move AI from pilot to production
Gold surges past $4,000/oz on geopolitical safe-haven demand; rupee weakens to 94.31 per dollar

daily_pulse.media_headline: Markets cautious as US-Iran tensions weigh on sentiment; FII outflows persist but domestic SIP flows provide structural floor near 24,000 Nifty support

opportunities: Domestic SIP flows at ₹30,954 Cr/month creating structural floor — retail participation re-rating mid-cap indices through 2030
PSU capex supercycle in railways, defence, and power driving earnings CAGR of 15–18% through 2029
India's MSCI EM Index weight rising steadily — passive FII allocation to accelerate once global rate cycle turns
risks: FII net selling of ₹45,121 Cr in June 2026 signals persistent foreign capital flight amid elevated US rates at 3.50–3.75%
Rupee depreciation to 94.31/USD eroding real returns for foreign investors; further weakness possible if Fed turns hawkish
Concentration risk in Nifty 50 — top 10 stocks represent 57% of weight, amplifying drawdowns in correction phases

opportunities: India GCCs cross 2,100 centers in 2026 — evolving from cost centres to AI-first product innovation hubs in Bengaluru and Hyderabad
Indian IT AI services revenue reaches $10–12B; 25% of firms now in AI production — margin expansion through automation gains
Indian SaaS ARR crosses $50B milestone; vertical SaaS and product-led growth strategies opening new export categories
risks: US H-1B policy tightening under new administration forcing onshore staffing cost inflation — eroding offshore arbitrage
Agentic AI commoditising routine coding and testing tasks — Tier-2 IT firms face headcount-dependent revenue model disruption
Client budget freezes in BFSI and retail verticals as ECB hikes rates and US recession risk lingers

opportunities: IndiaAI Mission deploying sovereign GPU clusters — common computing facilities democratising AI access for 5,000+ startups
Agentic AI adoption in BPO and KPO sectors: India positioned to capture $15B incremental AI-services market by 2028
Localised LLM fine-tuning in Indic languages (Hindi, Tamil, Telugu) opening 600M+ vernacular user addressable market
risks: NVIDIA H100/H200 export controls tightening — GPU delivery delays of 6–18 months threatening sovereign compute timelines
AI talent concentration: 80% of AI researchers clustered in Bengaluru and Hyderabad; salary inflation at 25–30% YoY
India's DPDP Act enforcement uncertainty could stall enterprise AI data-pipeline investments and cross-border data flows

opportunities: India poised to overtake Japan as 4th largest equity market by 2035 — MF AUM already at ₹81.58 lakh Cr and accelerating
Generational wealth transfer from real estate to financial assets underway — equity MF AUM to cross ₹200 Lakh Cr by 2035
Manufacturing renaissance under PLI 2.0 and semiconductor fab investments could add 3–4% to listed corporate earnings CAGR
risks: Climate-related physical risks (extreme heat, erratic monsoons) emerging as systemic threat to agriculture, power, and infra sectors
Geopolitical fragmentation (US-Iran, US-China) creating supply-chain bifurcation — India's export markets could narrow
Demographic dividend turning liability post-2038 without accelerated skilling and productivity gains in AI-adjacent domains

opportunities: India-headquartered AI software platforms (not just services) will emerge by 2032 — SaaS-to-PaaS transition creates new export class
Digital public infrastructure (UPI, ONDC, AA framework) becoming global export — 15+ countries adopting India Stack by 2033
Quantum computing infrastructure buildout creating new engineering discipline — India's IIT pipeline positioned to lead R&D
risks: Full agentic automation of software development by 2033–2035 could structurally eliminate junior-to-mid engineer roles
Brain drain acceleration if India-US visa pathways remain constrained — top AI talent migrating permanently to US, EU, and Singapore
Legacy COBOL/mainframe systems in global BFSI — India IT firms carrying technical debt risk as modernisation deadlines approach

opportunities: India's chip design ecosystem (Qualcomm, Intel, AMD design centres) maturing into sovereign IP creation by 2033
AGI-adjacent systems driving India's ₹5 Lakh Cr AI economy target — healthcare, agriculture, and judiciary automation as key verticals
Hyderabad and Chennai emerging as global AI inference data-centre hubs leveraging renewable energy and submarine cable access
risks: AGI capability overhang: if general-purpose AI reaches human parity by 2030, entire knowledge-worker economy requires structural redesign
Chip sovereignty gap — India lacks domestic foundry capability below 28nm; TSMC dependency remains single-point geopolitical failure
AI-driven misinformation at scale could destabilise retail investor sentiment and regulatory trust in financial markets
